finding: self-audit revision (self-audit): '"Claim 1 states it directly... quoting exactly as filed... \'comparing a statistical distribution... detecting an approaching of the edge of the cliff\' [0005]" -- and, later, "Claim 1 calls this whole family of changes \'identifying a convergence\' [0005]"' -> 'the claim-1 quote now carries the full granted claim-1 text, including "the comparing comprising identifying a convergence of an intensity of the reflected signals or of a median distance to a ground," attributed by name ("Claim 1 states it directly...") with no [dddd] bracket; the second, redundant "identifying a convergence" [0005] reference was removed (the idea is now covered once, correctly, in the fixed quote)'. both self-audit reviewers independently caught this, from different angles (Reviewer A checked the exact phrase against [0005] and found it absent; Reviewer B independently checked claim 1 as granted against the quoted text and found the quote was actually the [0005]/[0110]/Abstract boilerplate, missing claim 1's own disjunctive limitation). Verified byte-for-byte against input/patent.md before applying: [0005] does not contain "identifying a convergence" anywhere; that phrase exists only in the claims (청구항 0001항, 청구항 0008항), which are not [dddd]-paragraph-numbered in this document. This is the SAME finding class as run 045's first occurrence (see meta/findings-ledger.jsonl, essay_id 045-agility-638-last-mile-moat, pattern_tag claim-vs-spec-citation-conflation, currently status "watch" at count 1) -- this is occurrence #2 of the identical defect shape (verbatim claim language cited with a specification-paragraph [dddd] bracket instead of being attributed by claim number in prose), recommending pipeline-retro promote this class per RECUR_THRESHOLD.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit): '"FIG. 6 runs the equivalent check for the medium-range convergence test, and FIG. 7 covers the simplest of the three, whether the ground has been lost entirely. Two independent signals, checked against a live ambient reading rather than a fixed number, is what keeps \'the floor changed color\' from reading as \'the floor ended.\'"' -> 'FIG. 6 is now described as a distinct percentage-convergence test with no ambient-light component; FIG. 7\'s ambient-rate use is scoped to its actual conditional sub-case (the "distances increase" branch only); closing line changed to "Three separately-triggered tests, not one test run three times, is what keeps..."'. Reviewer B (skeptical pro-subject reader) flagged that "equivalent check" and "two independent signals... ambient reading" imply all three range-tests share one ambient-light-based mechanism. Verified against input/patent.md [0090]-[0091] (FIG. 5 / long range: genuine ambient-rate comparison), [0097]-[0100] (FIG. 6 / medium range: pure percentage-threshold convergence test, no ambient-rate involvement at all), and [0103]-[0106] (FIG. 7 / short range: ground-loss test, ambient-rate only required in the sub-case where distances increase). The original synthesis line overgeneralized three structurally distinct sub-tests into one shared mechanism.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit): '"**STM is not solving SLAM here. It is building the senses, and the reflex, that a SLAM stack has to stand on before it can do anything else.**"' -> '"**STM is not solving SLAM here. It is building one of the senses, and a reflex, a SLAM stack needs if it is going to trust the ground under it.**"'. Reviewer B judged that while the sentence avoids the literal banned phrase ("STM solves SLAM"), "has to stand on before it can do anything else" implies a SLAM stack architecturally depends on this one patent's specific reflex, which overstates the closing claim beyond what the patent supports (cliff/edge detection and SLAM are typically loosely-coupled subsystems in practice). This is a single-reviewer, judgment-level finding (not independently corroborated by Reviewer A, and not a byte-verifiable fact against the patent text the way the two deltas above are) but ties directly to essay-context.md's explicit hard guardrail against overclaiming STM's SLAM role, so it was applied as a minimal, low-risk, non-structural wording change rather than logged as watch-only. Flagging as a new class -- not yet in meta/attribution-table.md -- for pipeline-retro to route (goal 1 grounding-proportionality / thesis-spine adversarial-defense steelman-refine scope, tentatively).
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit-r2): '"1st 3D ToF LiDAR with 2.3K zones and flood illumination" (as an exact quote of ST\'s blog title)' -> '"1st 3D ToF LiDAR sensor with 2.3K zones and flood illumination" (word "sensor" restored)'. Reviewer B flagged a dropped word vs ST's actual blog title; independently confirmed via live web search (not hallucinated). Traced to essay-context.md's own transcription of the upstream series-context brief, carried unchanged through every stage until the first byte-level live-source check.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: self-audit revision (self-audit-r2): '"FIG. 3\'s graph is the same event turned into six lines... then all six lines jumping together the instant the sensor loses the near floor"' -> 'split into two paragraphs; medium-range convergence phase named explicitly; final jump correctly attributed to only the three median-distance lines'. verified against patent [0077] (names only the three median-distance lines as increasing) and against a direct visual read of fig-03.png; original sentence also skipped the medium-range convergence phase entirely.
recommendation: captured via the revision-delta channel; owner artifact + lever per attribution-table.

finding: Iter-1 grounding hard-gate breach: a quote verbatim-matching specification paragraph [0018] was cited as [0025] instead. Well-formed 4-digit anchor that resolves in-index, so gate_anchors passed; caught only by pass-3 fact-verification's byte-for-byte re-check against input/patent.md, not by any deterministic gate. [resolved iter 2: citation corrected [0025]->[0018], re-verified byte-for-byte.] Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim on disk; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: Re-anchor citations to the paragraph the quote actually appears in before finalizing a draft; per score-history.md, this was the sole substantive (non-cosmetic) defect of the run and did not recur after iter 1->2.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Iter-1 HIGH: 5 paragraphs exceeded the mobile-readability heuristic (words/12 > 8 mobile lines), gate-invisible to gate_structure's sentence-count-only STRUCT-001 check. Drove the iter-1 revise-required alongside the citation mis-anchor. [resolved iter1->2: two of the flagged paragraphs split cleanly; residual 5 more paragraphs still marginally over threshold carried into iter 2 as a medium.] Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: Same cross-run mobile-paragraph-wall recommendation: add a word-count warn heuristic (~>110w, i.e. STRUCT-005) to gate_structure and/or a mobile paragraph-length band to section-blueprint so Compose self-limits at draft time instead of costing iterations.

finding: Iter-1 medium: a [0086] anchor was misplaced (attached to the wrong claim/prose location relative to the paragraph it actually supports). [resolved iter 2: anchor repositioned.] Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: Same class as the [0025]/[0018] high finding in this round: verify each anchor resolves to the exact paragraph supporting the adjacent claim, not just that the bracket is well-formed and in-index.

finding: Iter-1 medium: an overclaim on [0036]'s content (prose asserted more than the paragraph's own "any quantity of rows may be included" language supports). [resolved iter 2: overclaim softened to stay within [0036]'s stated scope.] Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: Keep durability/generality claims tied to the source paragraph's own stated scope; do not extend beyond what the cited paragraph states.

finding: Iter-1: 2 pass-2 redundancy splits identified (paragraphs carrying overlapping sub-ideas that needed separating for compression/clarity). [resolved iter 2, folded into the same revision pass as the paragraph-length splits.] Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: Standard pass-2 compression guidance: split paragraphs carrying two distinct sub-ideas at the natural seam rather than letting them run together.

finding: Iter-1 low (first of 2 Sources-format observations): horizon-cluster Sources entries compressed to number + one-word descriptor rather than a fuller citation form. Reviewed and confirmed a deliberate, brief-mandated choice (essay-context.md instructs the horizon cluster be cited 'only as a horizon, a line or clause each'), not a defect; carried unchanged through all 4 rounds. Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: No change recommended; deliberate brief-mandated compression, re-confirmed each round through round 4.

finding: Iter-1 low (second of 2 Sources-format observations): hero/secondary patent Sources entries omit a publication date. Reviewed and confirmed invention-summary.md's own metadata section could not verify a publication date on this extraction; a disclosed gap, not a drafting lapse. Carried unchanged through all 4 rounds. Normalized from score-history.md's round-1 summary (edit-log.md round 1 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 2 ran).
recommendation: No change recommended; upstream metadata could not verify the field. If this recurs, consider having Phase 1 flag unverifiable date fields explicitly in invention-summary.md so Compose/Edit can cite the gap as sourced rather than silent.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Iter-2: citation fixes ([0025]->[0018], [0086] repositioned, [0036] overclaim softened) and the two flagged paragraph splits resolved both iter-1 highs cleanly, re-verified byte-for-byte. root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering) + essay-en-composer revision-mode re-scan (whole-essay recompute, not touched-region-only)
finding: Iter-3: 4 of the 5 iter-2-flagged paragraphs split successfully, but a fresh whole-essay recompute (not just the touched region) surfaced 2 more pre-existing marginal paragraphs, plus the steelman/SLAM-fencing paragraph which was deliberately left as one rhetorical unit rather than split. Third occurrence within this single run of the recurring mobile-paragraph-wall class (iter 1 -> iter 2 -> iter 3), still not fully resolved; revise-recommended again. Normalized from score-history.md's round-3 summary (edit-log.md round 3 not preserved verbatim; overwritten before round 4 ran).
recommendation: Same cross-run mobile-paragraph-wall recommendation (STRUCT-005 word-count warn), plus a revision-mode-specific note: a touched-region-only re-scan can miss pre-existing marginal paragraphs elsewhere in the essay; revision mode should re-scan the whole draft for this heuristic class, not just the region a fix touched (this run did so and caught it, but only because the process called for a fresh whole-essay recompute this round).

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Iter-4 (final, passing round): no findings — a script-verified sweep (word_count/12 > 8, i.e. >96 words) confirmed zero paragraphs over threshold across all 32 body paragraphs; the three named split boundaries from iter-3's revision were independently re-inspected fresh and found clean (no orphaned citation, awkward opener, redundancy, or logic gap). Maximum paragraph in the final essay body: 95 words / 7.92 mobile lines. Verbatim from edit-log.md (round 4, on disk). This is the 4th and final occurrence of the mobile-paragraph-wall class within this single run, now genuinely resolved (loop terminated at the iteration cap, 4/4, with a clean pass, not a forced stop).
recommendation: No further action for this run. Cross-run: the class recurred 3x (iter 1->2->3) before resolving at iter 4 within a single essay -- see the separate within-run-recurrence signal logged for pipeline-retro's attention.

root_cause_artifact: x-articles-format-en.md (Sources horizon-cluster compression) + thesis-architect invention-summary.md metadata (publication-date verification)

Both are disclosed, legitimate gaps, not drafting lapses.'

finding: Run-level coverage record: deterministic gates never failed in any of the 4 rounds of this run (all 11 gates passed=true every round; only warn-level STRUCT-004 rule-of-three false-positives and LONGSENT-001 long-sentence warns persisted, both judged pre-existing/accepted, not regressions). The editorial inner loop reached iteration 4 of the 4-iteration cap with overall_assessment: pass (0 high, 0 medium, 1 low). Recorded per ledger-schema.md's convention that empty-pass / coverage records prove a check ran and prevent mistaking silence for absence.
